_meta.description: Prohibited and restricted goods for import/export into Zimbabwe

prohibited_imports.reason: Absolute prohibition — criminal offence under Money Laundering and Proceeds of Crime Act
Zimbabwe signatory to Basel Convention on transboundary waste movement
prohibited_imports.penalty: Seizure, forfeiture, criminal prosecution, USD 50,000+ fine

prohibited_imports.reason: Prohibited under Censorship and Entertainment Controls Act Chapter 10:04
Banned under Rotterdam Convention; severe health hazard
Narcotics and Psychotropic Substances Act Chapter 15:12
prohibited_imports.penalty: Seizure, prosecution, imprisonment up to 20 years

prohibited_exports.reason: Kimberley Process — uncertified rough diamonds prohibited
CITES Convention; Parks and Wildlife Act Chapter 20:14
Basel Convention — prohibited transboundary hazardous waste export

restricted_imports.notes: Separate permits required for dealer, sports, security; private import heavily restricted
Mining explosives additionally require Ministry of Mines Inspectorate sign-off
Product must be MCAZ-registered; importer must be MCAZ-licensed
Dual-use chemicals; import quantity and end-use monitored
Passenger vehicles max 10 years; commercial max 15 years; must be RHD; EVs exempt from age limit
Montreal Protocol; annual quota assigned per importer

restricted_imports.permit_authority: Ministry of Agriculture — Biotechnology Authority of Zimbabwe
restricted_imports.notes: Zimbabwe has strict GMO controls; research permits available; food aid GMOs handled case-by-case

restricted_exports.notes: All gold must first be sold to Fidelity Gold Refinery (subsidiary of RBZ); retail gold export requires MMCZ permit

restricted_exports.permit_type: MMCZ Export Permit + Kimberley Process Certificate
restricted_exports.notes: KP certificate mandatory for all rough diamond exports
Zimbabwe is world's 2nd largest platinum producer; all PGM exports through MMCZ
Zimbabwe has Arcadia and Bikita lithium deposits; raw lithium export requires MMCZ; value addition encouraged
Zimbabwe has Great Dyke chrome belt; raw ore export restricted; ferrochrome export preferred
Policy restricts scrap exports to support local steel industry; ZISCO and other mills given priority access
Zimbabwe is major tobacco exporter; all leaf tobacco exports must be registered with TIMB and auctioned/contracted

restricted_exports.permit_authority: Zimbabwe Parks and Wildlife Management Authority
restricted_exports.notes: CITES listed species; elephant ivory trade subject to international moratorium

passenger_vehicles.notes: Age calculated from year of manufacture to year of import

commercial_vehicles.notes: Includes trucks, buses, and special purpose vehicles

electric_vehicles.notes: EVs are exempt from age restrictions per SI 157/2024 to encourage clean energy transition

vehicle_age_limits.drive_side_requirement: Right-hand drive (RHD) only — Zimbabwe drives on the left
